posts._note: Talk about the problem. No link in first 3-5 lines. Make them feel the frustration. Then reveal the guide.
posts.linkedin:
You're doing the work of 3 people.

Client delivery. Content creation. Admin. Outreach. Proposals. Follow-ups. Invoicing.

You started your solo business for freedom. But what you built is a 60-hour-a-week job — with yourself as the only employee.

AI tools are supposed to fix this. But instead of saving you time, they've added a new task: figuring out which tools to use, how to connect them, and why the thing you built last week broke this week.

Here's what most solopreneurs get wrong about AI automation: they treat it as a collection of tools instead of a system.

The A.U.T.O.M.A.T.E.™ Framework — the core of this guide — shows you how to build an integrated AI system that runs your repetitive tasks in the background while you focus on high-value work.

8 phases. 17 pages. $45.

posts._note: Show 1 key model or system inside the guide. Build authority. Teach something real.
posts.linkedin:
The 8-phase AI automation system for solopreneurs — built around one principle: automate the repetitive, own the irreplaceable.

The A.U.T.O.M.A.T.E.™ Framework:

→ A — Audit: Map every task you do in a week. Categorize by value vs. repeatability. The high-repeat, low-value tasks are your automation targets.

→ U — Unify: Connect your tools into a single workflow ecosystem. Stop using tools in isolation. Zapier, Make, or n8n as your connective layer.

→ T — Template: Build reusable AI prompt templates for your most frequent content and communication tasks. One good prompt replaces 30 minutes of work.

→ O — Orchestrate: Set up multi-step automations that trigger based on events. New lead → automated follow-up sequence → CRM update → task created.

→ M — Monetize: Use automation to scale delivery — not just reduce admin. AI-assisted services you deliver faster = more clients at the same effort.

→ A — Analyze: Track time saved weekly. Identify bottlenecks. Every automation has an ROI — measure it.

→ T — Tier: Build automation in layers. Basic first (email templates, scheduling), then advanced (content pipelines, lead nurturing), then fully autonomous (24/7 workflows).

→ E — Expand: Add new automation layers as your business grows. The system scales with you.

$45. 17 pages. Real tool stack included.

👉 https://publixion.com/guides/ai-automation-solopreneurs

#AI #Automation #Solopreneur #Publixion

posts._note: Show how a specific person would use this guide step by step. LinkedIn + Facebook only.
posts.linkedin:
Here's how a freelance content strategist used this guide to reclaim 12 hours per week.

Before the guide: Working 55 hours a week. Admin, outreach, proposals, content scheduling, and client reporting eating half her time.

Phase A (Audit): She mapped every task in a week and scored each on value vs. repeatability. Identified 6 high-repeat, low-value processes: proposal drafting, client reporting, content scheduling, lead follow-up, invoice reminders, and social posting.

Phase U (Unify): Connected HubSpot, Notion, and Gmail through Zapier as her central automation layer.

Phase T (Template): Built AI prompt templates for proposal drafts and weekly client reports. What used to take 2 hours each now takes 20 minutes.

Phase O (Orchestrate): Set up a trigger: new lead in HubSpot → automated 3-email follow-up sequence → Notion task created for call booking.

Phase M (Monetize): With 12 hours freed per week, she took on 2 additional clients at her existing rate.

Result: Same working hours. 2 more clients. 40% revenue increase.

The guide includes the exact Zapier workflow setup and prompt templates she used.

👉 https://publixion.com/guides/ai-automation-solopreneurs

#AIAutomation #CaseStudy #Solopreneur #Publixion

posts.linkedin:
"$45 for a guide feels like a lot."

If this guide saves you 10 hours a week — and your time is worth even $25/hour — that's $250 in recovered capacity per week. The guide pays for itself in under 5 hours of recovered time.

"I'm not technical enough to set up automations."
The guide uses tools with visual interfaces (Zapier, Make, Notion). No code. The guide includes step-by-step setup walkthroughs with screenshots. If you can drag and drop, you can build these workflows.

"I've already tried AI tools and they didn't save me time."
Because you used tools — not a system. The A.U.T.O.M.A.T.E.™ Framework starts with an audit of what to automate first, which is what most people skip. Wrong automation = more complexity, not less.

"My business is too specific for generic automation advice."
The framework is a methodology, not a rigid script. The Audit phase helps you identify your specific automation opportunities. The tool recommendations are proven — but adaptable.

"What if my workflow changes?"
The guide includes a tiered approach. Basic automations first. You build up layers as your confidence grows.

👉 https://publixion.com/guides/ai-automation-solopreneurs
